title: Cleisostoma striolatum

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma striolatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. taxonomy: Cleisostoma striolatum is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. Within this family, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Aeridinae. This orchid is further classified as belonging to the genus Cleisostoma, a genus of small, delicate orchids. The species is native to Southeast Asia and is characterized by its delicate white flowers with yellow markings. Cleisostoma striolatum is a rare and lesser-known species within the orchid family.
